name: New Caledonia (Nouvelle-Calédonie / Kanaky)
summary: New Caledonia is a Pacific Island civil-law sui generis collectivity of the French Republic under the Organic Law 99-209 of 19 March 1999 (Statut de la Nouvelle-Calédonie) implementing the 1998 Nouméa Accord — structurally distinctive globally as the only sui generis collectivity within the French Republic with constitutionally-protected partial sovereignty and customary-status framework. Family-law framework operates under the French Civil Code (Code Civil) with substantial Kanak customary-status (statut civil coutumier kanak) framework — constitutionally-recognised parallel civil-status regime for Kanak indigenous-status persons. Parental authority and child custody operate under Civil Code arts. 371-387 (common-law civil-status) or under Kanak customary-law (customary-status). The Court of Appeal of Nouméa is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction was retained with the French Court of Cassation in Paris. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Tribunal of First Instance of Nouméa (Tribunal de Première Instance) and on customary-status matters via Customary Sessions (formation coutumière). Psychology profession is regulated through the French Republic professional framework as applicable in New Caledonia. New Caledonia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. New Caledonia is a Hague Convention 1980 party via French Republic territorial extension effective 1 December 1983.

statutory_framework.citation: French Civil Code arts. 371-387 (applicable in New Caledonia)
statutory_framework.title: French Civil Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: French Civil Code applicable to common-law civil-status (statut civil de droit commun) persons in New Caledonia. Arts. 371-387 govern autorité parentale and child custody.

statutory_framework.citation: Organic Law 99-209 of 19 March 1999 (Statut de la Nouvelle-Calédonie)
statutory_framework.title: Statute of New Caledonia
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Organic Law implementing 1998 Nouméa Accord establishing New Caledonia's sui generis collectivity status with constitutionally-protected partial sovereignty.

statutory_framework.title: Kanak customary-status framework
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitutionally-recognised Kanak customary-status (statut civil coutumier kanak) for Kanak indigenous-status persons operating in parallel with French civil-law.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Nouméa (Cour d'appel de Nouméa)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: French Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from New Caledonia.

professional_regulators.name: French Republic professional framework (applicable in New Caledonia)
professional_regulators.role: French professional regulatory framework for health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology applicable in New Caledonia.

anonymisation_convention: New Caledonian family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Appeal practice using initials.

key_developments.title: French annexation of New Caledonia
key_developments.description: Admiral Auguste Febvrier-Despointes formally annexed New Caledonia for France on 24 September 1853 — beginning of French sovereignty + foundation for subsequent colonial-administrative-then-sui-generis framework.

key_developments.title: Kanak Revolt of 1878 + colonial violence framework
key_developments.description: Kanak Revolt of 1878 led by Chief Ataï June-December 1878 — first major Kanak resistance to French colonisation — ~1,200 Kanak and ~200 French casualties — pivotal pre-modern foundation for subsequent Kanak indigenous-rights framework and modern customary-status framework consolidation.

key_developments.title: Citizenship extension + colonial-to-overseas-territory transition
key_developments.description: French citizenship extended to all Kanak residents 1946 under post-WWII French Union framework — pivotal transition from colonial to overseas-territory framework + foundation for subsequent indigenous-rights consolidation.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by France to New Caledonia effective 1 December 1983 — applies to common-law civil-status (statut civil de droit commun) framework + bilateral framework with Kanak customary-status framework.

key_developments.title: Matignon-Oudinot Accords
key_developments.description: Matignon-Oudinot Accords of 26 June 1988 between French government and pro-independence FLNKS and anti-independence RPCR — pivotal post-Ouvéa-cave-massacre framework establishing 10-year transitional autonomy framework + foundation for subsequent 1998 Nouméa Accord.

key_developments.title: Nouméa Accord
key_developments.description: Nouméa Accord of 5 May 1998 establishing path toward New Caledonian self-determination including constitutional Kanak customary-status recognition — pivotal modern foundation framework for sui generis collectivity status + foundation for subsequent three independence referendums framework.

key_developments.title: Statute of New Caledonia (Organic Law 99-209)
key_developments.description: Federal Organic Law 99-209 of 19 March 1999 implementing 1998 Nouméa Accord establishing New Caledonia's sui generis collectivity status with constitutionally-protected partial sovereignty — structurally distinctive globally only sui generis collectivity within French Republic with constitutionally-protected partial sovereignty and customary-status framework.

key_developments.title: First independence referendum
key_developments.description: First independence referendum 4 November 2018 — 56.7% maintain current status / 43.3% independence — pivotal first of three Nouméa-Accord-mandated referendums.

key_developments.title: Third independence referendum (boycotted)
key_developments.description: Third independence referendum 12 December 2021 — 96.5% maintain current status (boycotted by independence movement) — pivotal final Nouméa-Accord-mandated referendum + dispute over French government's refusal to delay referendum to mourning period for COVID-19 deaths.

key_developments.title: Constitutional electoral reform unrest + Bougival Accord context
key_developments.description: May 2024 unrest in New Caledonia following French National Assembly Constitutional Bill electoral roll reform proposal — violent protests + 13 deaths + state of emergency + significant pivotal context for ongoing sui generis collectivity status framework + subsequent November-December 2024 Bougival Accord negotiations + continuing constitutional reform framework.

key_developments.title: Cour d'appel de Nouméa + Cour de Cassation + customary-Kanak framework — interest-of-the-child substantive register
key_developments.description: Court of Appeal of Nouméa and French Court of Cassation continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code arts. 371-387 + Statute of Nouméa Accord 1998 + customary-Kanak framework (Civil Status of Personal Law) in custody disputes within continuing sui generis collectivity status framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption — within French Pacific sui generis collectivity + dual personal-status (common Civil Code + customary Kanak) framework.

structural_findings: New Caledonia operates a structurally distinctive globally sui generis collectivity framework within French Republic — constitutionally-recognised parallel Kanak customary-status (statut civil coutumier kanak) operating alongside French civil-law (statut civil de droit commun).

Constitutional recognition of Kanak customary-status with parallel personal-status jurisdiction is structurally distinctive globally — only sui generis collectivity within French Republic in the corpus.
Three independence referendums (2018, 2020, 2021) all resulted in maintaining current status — structurally distinctive within the corpus.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via French Republic territorial extension reflects sui generis collectivity Hague jurisdiction status.
editorial_notes: New Caledonia jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — civil-law Pacific French sui generis collectivity (1853 French annexation + 1878 Kanak Revolt Chief Ataï + 1946 French citizenship extension + 1983 Hague Convention territorial extension + 1988 Matignon-Oudinot Accords + 1998 Nouméa Accord + 1999 Organic Law 99-209 + 2018 first independence referendum + 2021 third referendum + 2024 constitutional electoral reform unrest + Bougival Accord context). Only sui generis collectivity within French Republic globally with constitutionally-protected partial sovereignty + Kanak customary-status framework + three Nouméa-Accord-mandated independence referendums all maintaining current status + 2024 unrest 13-death state-of-emergency context.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Pacific Island + French-civil-law + sui-generis-collectivity-globally-distinctive cluster + Kanak-customary-status-constitutional-recognition + Hague-via-French-Republic-territorial-extension clusters within the corpus.
